steps.title: Set up the system
steps.content: We have two linear equations: $y=-\frac{1}{8}x$ and $y=\frac{1}{10}x$. To find the value of x that solves this system, we set the expressions for y equal to each other: \(-\frac{1}{8}x = \frac{1}{10}x$.

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
\( -\frac{1}{8}x = \frac{1}{10}x$
Adding $\frac{1}{8}x$ to both sides, we get: \$0 = \left(\frac{1}{10} + \frac{1}{8}\right)x = \frac{8+10}{80}x = \frac{18}{80}x$.
This simplifies to $0 = \frac{9}{40}x$ which means $x = 0$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
